entities.name: CEREAL CROPS
entities.description: General category of crops analyzed for transpiration response

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental stress characterized by lack of water

entities.name: WATER CONSERVATIVE TRAITS
entities.description: Inherited and adaptive characteristics that help plants conserve water

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The rate at which water is lost from plant leaves to the atmosphere

entities.name: VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: The difference between the amount of moisture in the air and how much moisture the air can hold when it is saturated

entities.name: SOIL WATER CONSERVATION
entities.description: The process of reducing the loss of water from the soil

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and productivity during drought

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the leaves of a plant

entities.name: C4 CEREALS
entities.description: Cereal crops that use the C4 photosynthetic pathway

relationships.description: Plant adaptation to drought depends on water conservative traits
Transpiration rate is expressed under high vapor pressure deficit conditions
Limited transpiration rate is a potential source of soil water conservation
Soil water conservation contributes to drought tolerance
Maize is a type of C4 cereal
Pearl millet is a type of C4 cereal
Sorghum is a type of C4 cereal
Genetic variation for limited transpiration rate identified in maize
Genetic variation for limited transpiration rate identified in pearl millet
Genetic variation for limited transpiration rate identified in sorghum
Maize was analyzed under elevating vapor pressure deficit
Pearl millet was analyzed under elevating vapor pressure deficit
Sorghum was analyzed under elevating vapor pressure deficit
Water transpired under elevating VPD was not found dependent on leaf area but on transpiration rate
Pearl millet showed better adaptation of limitation of transpiration rate than maize and sorghum
